slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log establishes a formal, time-bound control closure requirement for Q1 2024 vendor reviews, mandating complete evidence archiving with strict ownership and linkage to control periods, while the Privileged Account Approval Email governs conditional, time-bound access for specific personnel with quarterly revalidation obligations tied to vendor accountability.

- Access Review Evidence Log requires permanent archival of evidence in an approved repository to close controls; Privileged Account Approval is time-bound and requires quarterly revalidation.  
- Access Review Evidence Log mandates explicit linkage of evidence to control periods and ownership; Privileged Account Approval ties access to specific personnel and operational scope.  
- Access Review Evidence Log prohibits final control closure if evidence is missing or pending; Privileged Account Approval requires revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- Access Review Evidence Log is a formal evidence register for control validation; Privileged Account Approval is an operational email with conditional governance terms.  
- Access Review Evidence Log applies to vendor-control reviews broadly; Privileged Account Approval targets specific administrator access within a pilot program.  
- Access Review Evidence Log is synthetic and for testing; Privileged Account Approval is an actual governance action with compliance conditions.
